commit.message:
perf: optimize pathfinding hot paths in simulator (#109)

- Replace Math.pow(x, 4) with manual exponentiation (x²·x²) in BPR
  congestion model — eliminates expensive Math.pow calls per A* edge
  evaluation in both main thread and worker
- Replace O(n²) unshift() with push()+reverse() in route reconstruction
  for both RoadNetwork and pathfinding worker
- Cache incident edge fingerprint (sort+join) — invalidated only on
  incident changes instead of recomputed per route lookup

arch: restructure monorepo into domain-driven packages (#48)

* Initial plan

* feat: create 7 new domain-driven monorepo packages

Add the following packages as part of a domain-driven monorepo restructure:

- packages/domain (@financialadvisor/domain): Standalone core types and
  utilities (types.ts, utils.ts from shared). No internal dependencies.

- packages/ledger (@financialadvisor/ledger): Account integration types,
  the AccountIntegrationService class, and savings/goal planning tools.
  Depends on domain. The createAccountIntegrationService factory (which
  depends on concrete file importers) is moved to ingestion to avoid
  circular dependencies.

- packages/ingestion (@financialadvisor/ingestion): CSV and OFX/QFX file
  importers plus the createAccountIntegrationService factory that wires
  them together. Depends on domain and ledger.

- packages/resolution (@financialadvisor/resolution): Transaction
  categorization and analysis. Depends on domain.

- packages/analytics (@financialadvisor/analytics): Budget analysis,
  investment portfolio tools, and predictive analytics. Depends on domain.

- packages/advice (@financialadvisor/advice): Stub package for future
  financial advice capabilities. Depends on domain and analytics.

- packages/ai-providers (@financialadvisor/ai-providers): AI provider
  implementations (OpenAI, Ollama, Copilot), provider manager, accuracy
  enhancer, performance optimizer, production monitor, and financial
  planning agent. Depends on domain. Uses axios ^1.13.5 (patched for
  CVE-2025 SSRF and DoS vulnerabilities vs. 1.7.2).

All packages use "type": "module", ESM exports, and composite TypeScript
project references. Internal @financialadvisor/* imports updated from
@financialadvisor/shared to @financialadvisor/domain. Relative imports
within each package preserved.

commit.message:
Resilient tracked-path handling, --pedantic, --quiet, and file logging (#2)

* Warn instead of fail on tracked paths, add --pedantic, --quiet, and file logging

Tracked files in .worktreeinclude caused hooks to exit non-zero, breaking
Claude Code worktree creation. The default behavior now warns and skips
tracked paths (they're already in the worktree via Git). Use --pedantic
to restore the strict fail-on-tracked behavior.

Both implementations now log all output to worktree.log in the current
directory with timestamps and levels. --quiet suppresses stderr while
keeping the log file, useful for hook mode debugging.

Tests: 98 assertions (was 86), covering warn-on-tracked, --pedantic,
and --quiet for both Python and Bash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: log file validation, test isolation, README clarity

- --quiet + unwritable log file is now fatal (both implementations),
  preventing silent output loss
- test_create_quiet runs from a dedicated temp cwd and asserts
  worktree.log exists with content

commit.message:
fix(ol-dbt-cli): eliminate false positives in broken_ref_columns and upstream_refs (#2022)

Four root causes of false positive validation errors identified and fixed:

1. Seeds not indexed in manifest.by_name
   - Seeds (resource_type='seed') were stored in registry.nodes but never added to
     registry.by_name, so manifest.get_model() could not find them.
   - Fix: index seeds in by_name alongside models in load_manifest().
   - Effect: upstream_refs no longer warns for ref()s to seeds that have column
     definitions in schema YAMLs (e.g. platforms, user_course_roles,
     legacy_edx_certificate_revision_mapping).

2. UNNEST lateral join columns attributed to upstream ref
   - 'cross join unnest(...) as t(col)' creates an unqualified column 'col'.
   - Pass 2 of get_columns_read_from_ref() skips attribution when a non-passthrough
     regular JOIN is present, but j.find(exp.Table) returns None for an UNNEST node
     so the guard never triggered, wrongly attributing UNNEST aliases to the upstream.
   - Fix: extend the join guard to also fire when j.find(exp.Unnest) is not None.
   - Affected models: int__mitxpro__coursesinprogram, int__mitxpro__coursesfaculty,
     int__mitxpro__programsfaculty, int__ocw__course_departments,
     int__ocw__course_instructors.

3. Unaliased qualified columns from JOIN tables attributed to upstream
   - Pattern: 'select upstream.*, other.col1 from upstream join other'.
   - The passthrough CTE detection collected aliases (exp.Alias) and bare Star nodes
     but skipped unaliased qualified columns (exp.Column with a table qualifier).
     So 'other.col1' was never added to passthrough_added_cols[cte], and downstream
     reads of 'col1' were incorrectly attributed to upstream.
   - Fix: in the passthrough CTE added-column collection loop, capture unaliased
     qualified columns whose qualifier is NOT in passthrough_aliases.
   - Affected models: int__edxorg__mitx_courseruns, edxorg_to_mitxonline_users.

4. Passthrough chain does not propagate added columns across hops
   - Pattern: CTE A (passthrough + adds hash_col), CTE B (select * from CTE A).
   - Pass 1 checked cte_added = passthrough_added_cols.get(table_name) for the
     *immediate* CTE alias, so accessing B.hash_col looked up B's added set which
     didn't include hash_col (only A's added set did).
   - Fix: replace per-CTE cte_added lookup with passthrough_computed (the union of
     all added cols across all passthrough CTEs in the query) for Pass 1 exclusion.
   - Affected model: int__edxorg__mitx_program_certificates.

Tests added for all four patterns in test_impact.py (TestGetColumnsReadFromRef) and
test_validate.py (TestUpstreamRefsSeedResolution).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(ui): fade in POIs and speed limits after zoom settles

Hide POIs and speed limit signs during active zoom gestures and
fade them in via deck.gl enter-transitions once the viewport
stabilises. Introduces useSettledZoom hook that debounces the raw
zoom value (300ms) and exposes an isZooming flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): use PAT for release-please to trigger CI on release PR

The release-please action was using the default GITHUB_TOKEN to push
version-bump commits to the release branch. GitHub's security policy
prevents GITHUB_TOKEN pushes from triggering workflow runs, so CI
never ran on release PR #81 (0 check runs, perpetually pending).

This meant the release PR could not be merged (branch protection
requires passing checks), so release_created was never true and
docker-publish was always skipped.

Using a PAT (RELEASE_TOKEN secret) allows the push to trigger CI
workflows on the release branch, unblocking the merge and the
subsequent Docker image publish.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
